# The Modh Playbook

> Battle-tested engineering patterns from production SaaS development by Modh Labs.
> 24 chapters across 7 categories. 18 AI agent skills.
> Stack: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Supabase, Clerk, Vercel.

## About Modh Labs

Modh Labs is an engineering studio that builds production SaaS products for high-growth companies. We believe in engineering excellence as a competitive advantage. This playbook encodes our patterns, standards, and hard-won lessons from shipping software at scale.

## Chapters

### Data Layer
- The Repository Pattern: All database access through typed repositories with generated types — never raw queries in components or actions
- Server Actions: Type-safe mutations with Zod validation, structured error responses, and automatic cache invalidation via revalidatePath
- Data Fetching: Server Components with async/await replace useEffect and client-side state management entirely
- Database Standards: Schema-first workflow where the database schema is the source of truth, types are generated, and Row Level Security is enforced on every table

### Architecture
- Route Colocation: Components, actions, and hooks live next to the route that uses them — shared only when three or more routes need them
- Webhook Architecture: SOLID handler registry with one handler per event type, dependency injection, idempotency keys, and structured error responses
- Multi-Tenant Isolation: Clerk handles auth and organizations, Supabase RLS policies enforce org boundaries at the database level, JWT contains org_id

### Quality
- TypeScript Strict: Zero any types, strict mode always enabled, database types generated from schema — never hand-written interfaces
- Testing Strategy: Vitest for unit and integration tests, Playwright for end-to-end flows, Supabase client mocking for repository tests
- CI Pipeline: Cheapest checks run first (lint, typecheck, then test), CI only validates — never deploys, fail fast on first error

### Security
- Row Level Security: Every table has RLS policies, no exceptions — policies read org_id from JWT, service role bypasses for webhooks only
- Input Validation: Zod schemas at every boundary — form submissions, server actions, webhook payloads, API endpoints
- Security Headers: Content Security Policy, CORS configuration, webhook signature verification with timing-safe comparison

### Observability
- Structured Logging: Module-scoped logger factory with context fields — never console.log in production code
- Error Tracking: Sentry with domain-specific capture functions that attach business context (user, org, entity IDs) — not generic exception logging
- Webhook Observability: Every webhook handler traced end-to-end with searchable tags for event type, handler, organization, and entity IDs

### Process
- Feature Design: Think before you code — brainstorm 2-3 approaches, write a design spec, get alignment, then implement
- Linear Tickets: User stories with acceptance criteria, architecture context for the implementer, sub-task breakdown for complex work
- Pull Requests: CI must pass before review, rich descriptions with summary and test plan, no force-push to shared branches
- Documentation: Three-layer system — README for humans, AGENTS.md for AI agents, inline docs for implementation context

### Frontend Craft
- Component Architecture: Server Components by default, client boundaries pushed to interactive leaves, composition over configuration props
- Performance Patterns: Suspense streaming for progressive loading, skeleton-first design, useOptimistic for instant UI feedback, smart prefetching
- Design Standards: shadcn/ui component library, semantic design tokens for automatic dark mode, cn() utility for conditional classes
- Internal Tools: Data density over visual impact, monospace numbers for scannability, dark mode default, minimal animation

## Agent Skills

18 portable AI coding agent skills that enforce these patterns automatically. Skills are organized in four tiers:

- Tier 1 Universal (4 skills): design-taste, internal-tools-design, output-enforcement, cross-editor-setup
- Tier 2 React/Next.js (3 skills): react-architecture, nextjs-patterns, shadcn-components
- Tier 3 Backend (5 skills): supabase-patterns, observability, webhook-architecture, security-and-compliance, testing
- Tier 4 Process (6 skills): doc-audit, feature-design, linear-tickets, pull-request, ci-pipeline, route-colocation

Compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, and OpenAI Codex.

- Website: https://modh.ca/playbook
